common.name: Name
components.sudoersSetupPanel.generatedFi: This file is generated from the commands configured for this system, including active custom script overrides.
components.sudoersSetupPanel.rootUserGui: The generated sudoers file is still shown as a reference, but using a dedicated non-root SSH account with these limited rules is recommended.
components.systemForm.approvedHostKeyDoe: The approved host key does not match the current host key.
components.systemForm.approvedHostKeySto: Approved host key stored for this system.
components.systemForm.approvedHostKeyWil: Approved host key will be saved with this system.
components.systemForm.autoHideKeptBackPa: Automatically move kept-back APT updates into the hidden-updates list after refreshes.
components.systemForm.completeSshHostKey: Complete SSH host-key approval above before running a connection test.
components.systemForm.fetchCurrentHostKe: Fetch the current host key to review and approve it here. Test Connection becomes available after host-key approval is complete.
components.systemForm.hideFromDashboardD: Keeps this system available in Systems, but hides it from the dashboard, notifications, and Upgrade All.
components.systemForm.hostKeyVerificatio: Host-key verification is disabled for this system. Connections to this host will not verify the SSH server identity.
components.systemForm.hostPresentedDiffe: The host presented a different key than the one currently approved. Approve it only if you expected this change.
components.systemForm.noApprovedSshHostK: No approved SSH host key stored.
components.systemForm.noSshCredentialsAv: No SSH credentials are available yet. Create one here without leaving this dialog.
components.systemForm.proxyJumpDescripti: Uses the selected system as a live SSH jump host chain.
components.systemForm.proxyJumpLoopbackS: Use a host or IP that the jump host can reach instead of loopback.
components.systemForm.refreshAppstreamDa: Disabling this skips the appstream refresh step and only checks for updates with current local metadata.
components.systemForm.refreshMetadataDur: Uses `dnf check-update --refresh` to force a metadata refresh during update checks.
components.systemForm.refreshPackageData: Disabling this skips the `pacman -Sy` refresh step and uses locally cached sync data only.
components.systemForm.refreshPackageInde: Disabling this skips `apk update` during checks and only lists updates from the current local index state.
components.systemForm.reviewAllFetchedHo: Review all {count} fetched host keys below. Each one must match what you expect before you approve.
components.systemForm.reviewFetchedHostK: Review the fetched host key below and approve it only if these details match what you expect for this host.
components.systemForm.saveWithoutHostKey: SSH host-key verification is enabled, but no host key has been approved yet. You can save now, but SSH actions will require host-key approval later from this dialog.
components.systemForm.storedApprovalNoLo: Stored approval no longer matches this host.
components.systemForm.storedSshHostKeyNo: The stored SSH host key no longer matches what this host presented during the latest check. Review and re-approve the current host key before running SSH actions again.
components.systemForm.sudoPasswordHelp: Only needed if the sudo password differs from the SSH credential password
components.systemForm.testConnectionDesc: Tests the connection and detects available package managers.
components.systemForm.verifySshHostKeyDe: Enabled by default for new systems. Disabling this weakens SSH host-key verification for this system only.
pages.credentials.areYouSureYouWantToDel: Are you sure you want to delete this credential? This action cannot be undone.
pages.credentials.credentialIsInUseAndCa: Credential is in use and cannot be deleted

pages.dashboard.deleteNameSystemsInThisG: Delete {name}? Systems in this group will move to Ungrouped.
pages.dashboard.dragGroupsAndSystemsToSe: Drag groups and systems to set the saved upgrade order. Systems in the same group start together; the next group waits for the previous group to finish.
pages.dashboard.dragToSetUpgradeGroupAnd: Drag to set upgrade group and order
pages.dashboard.noSystemsAreAvailableToG: No systems are available to group yet.
pages.dashboard.noSystemsHaveUpdatesRigh: No systems have updates right now. Enable edit mode to organize all systems.
pages.dashboard.toggleAndSaveFullUpgrade: Toggle and save full upgrade for this system
pages.login.oidcOnlyPasskey: This passkey belongs to an SSO-only account. Sign in with SSO instead.
pages.login.passkeysRequireASecureContex: Passkeys require a secure context (HTTPS or localhost)
pages.notifications.appUpdateEntityVisib: The app update entity is visibility-only. Per-system entities use synthetic fingerprint versions for the current pending update set, not real package version pairs.
pages.notifications.areYouSureYouWantToD: Are you sure you want to delete this notification channel? This action cannot be undone.
pages.notifications.enableHomeAssistantD: Enable Home Assistant discovery and retained state sync
pages.notifications.enableHomeAssistantI: Enable Home Assistant install commands for per-system entities
pages.notifications.eventsAreBatchedAndS: Events are batched and sent when any selected schedule runs.
pages.notifications.homeAssistantDiscove: Home Assistant discovery and retained state sync update immediately and are not affected by scheduled delivery.
pages.notifications.name: Name

pages.notifications.publishNotificationE: Publish notification events to a topic
pages.notifications.smtpAllowInsecureTls: Disabled by default. Use this only for exceptional self-signed SMTP endpoints you explicitly trust.
pages.notifications.smtpSecurityHelp: Use Plain SMTP for unencrypted relays on port 25, STARTTLS for ports like 587, and SMTPS / Implicit TLS for port 465. Prefer NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS for trusted private CAs.
pages.notifications.telegram.botCommands: Disabled by default. When enabled, the dashboard auto-generates a dedicated writable API token for this Telegram channel after the private chat is linked.
pages.notifications.telegram.botTokenHel: Create the bot with BotFather, then paste the bot token here.
pages.notifications.telegram.commandsCan: Telegram bot commands cannot authenticate right now. Reissue the token to restore command access.
pages.notifications.telegram.linkChatFir: Link the Telegram private chat first. The command token is issued only after the chat is linked.
pages.notifications.telegram.openLinkToB: Open this link in Telegram to bind the private chat:
pages.notifications.telegram.saveFirstTo: Save this notification first, then reopen it to create the one-time Telegram connect link.
pages.notifications.telegram.saveToActiv: Save this notification to activate Telegram commands and issue the dedicated command token.
pages.notifications.thisClearsTheStoredU: This clears the stored update-version dedupe state for this notification channel only. The next matching update check can notify again for currently available updates.
pages.notifications.updateDedupeResetFor: Update dedupe reset for notification channel
pages.notifications.updateDedupeResetIsO: Update dedupe reset is only available for channels subscribed to updates
pages.notifications.webhook.allowInsecur: Disabled by default. Only use this for exceptional self-signed endpoints you explicitly trust.
pages.notifications.webhook.queryParamet: Query parameters are intentionally not secret. URLs are more likely to be logged by receivers, proxies, and monitoring systems.
pages.notifications.webhook.templatesSup: Templates support simple dotted event paths like
pages.notifications.webhook.warning.meta: This destination is blocked server-side because it targets cloud metadata endpoints.
pages.notifications.webhook.warning.priv: This destination appears to be loopback, link-local, or private. That is allowed for trusted self-hosted targets, but treat it as an advanced setting.
pages.schedules.areYouSureYouWantToDelet: Are you sure you want to delete this schedule? This action cannot be undone.
pages.schedules.enterValidCronBeforeGene: Enter a valid cron expression before generating a name
pages.schedules.name: Name
pages.schedules.runsMoreOftenThanTheSupp: Runs more often than the supported {minutes} minute minimum.
pages.scripts.builtinParserNotice: {manager} update output is parsed by the built-in parser. Custom parser rules are only needed for custom package managers.
pages.scripts.builtinSystemInfoParserDes: This script will use the same OS, Proxmox/Raspberry Pi, uptime, kernel, and reboot-required parsing logic as the built-in system-info script.
pages.scripts.configDescription.autoAcce: true when checks may import new signing keys
pages.scripts.configDescription.autoHide: true when kept-back updates are auto-hidden
pages.scripts.configDescription.refreshA: true unless appstream refresh is disabled
pages.scripts.configDescription.refreshD: true unless database refresh is disabled
pages.scripts.configDescription.refreshI: true unless index refresh is disabled
pages.scripts.deleteNameSummaryItCannotB: Delete {name}? {summary}. It cannot be deleted until those systems use another script.
pages.scripts.deleteNameThisActionCannot: Delete {name}? This action cannot be undone.
pages.scripts.deleteScriptsBlockedIfAssi: Deletion will be blocked if any of these scripts are assigned to active systems.
pages.scripts.detectionScriptsUseOneComm: Detection scripts use one command. It must exit with 0 and print found when the package manager is available.
pages.scripts.error.configKeyCollides: {key} collides with a built-in {manager} config key.
pages.scripts.error.configKeyPattern: Custom config keys must start with a letter and use only letters, numbers, underscores, or dashes.
pages.scripts.error.detectionScriptsUseO: Detection scripts use exactly one step
pages.scripts.error.eachStepNeedsLabelAn: Each step needs a label and command
pages.scripts.error.exitCodes: Exit codes must be comma-separated non-negative integers
pages.scripts.error.importMissingPackage: Import file is missing package manager details
pages.scripts.error.parseStep: Parse step must be a non-negative number
pages.scripts.examplesFolderDescription: The examples folder contains various custom package manager examples.
pages.scripts.installedPackageParserDesc: Use named regex groups to turn the selected step output into installed-package rows. Required groups are packageName and currentVersion.
pages.scripts.issueDetectionDescription: Match check output that should show a package-manager warning and enable this repair action.
pages.scripts.managerKeyAlreadyExists: This manager key already exists. Choose a different key.
pages.scripts.managerKeysRenameGuidance: Keys are used by scripts and systems, so rename by creating a new manager.
pages.scripts.noConfigKeysForPackageMana: No config keys are defined for this package manager yet.
pages.scripts.noSupportCustomPackageMana: No support will be given for custom package managers.
pages.scripts.noSupportCustomScripts: No support will be given for custom scripts.
pages.scripts.packageManagerDeleteRequir: This package manager can only be deleted when it is disabled or no longer detected on every system.
pages.scripts.packageManagerImportedCrea: Package manager imported: {created} scripts created, {updated} updated
pages.scripts.packageManagerKeyKeyAlread: Package manager key "{key}" already exists. Choose a different key.
pages.scripts.packageManagerHasNoScripts: This package manager has no scripts.
pages.scripts.packageManagersDescription: Built-ins can be extended with custom config entries; custom managers can also be labeled.
pages.scripts.placeholder.manager: The package manager key for the current operation.
pages.scripts.placeholder.package: The first selected package name, validated before execution.
pages.scripts.placeholder.packages: All selected package names joined by spaces after validation.
pages.scripts.placeholder.quotedPackage: The first selected package shell-quoted with single quotes.
pages.scripts.placeholder.quotedPackages: All selected packages shell-quoted and joined by spaces.
pages.scripts.placeholder.sudoCommand: Wraps COMMAND with the dashboard sudo fallback helper.
pages.scripts.runRepairThenRefresh: Run this repair action, then refresh updates.
pages.scripts.runtime.autoremove.exitCod: A non-zero exit code marks the autoremove operation as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.autoremove.outputC: Output is streamed live, stored in history, and followed by a recheck.
pages.scripts.runtime.autoremove.parserB: No parser configuration is used while removing unused packages.
pages.scripts.runtime.autoremove.purpose: Removes packages or runtimes that are no longer needed.
pages.scripts.runtime.autoremove.stepBeh: The autoremove command runs as the operation body for the selected manager.
pages.scripts.runtime.builtinParserOutpu: Built-in parser chooses the required command output from the completed steps.
pages.scripts.runtime.check_updates.exit: Built-in parsers and custom success/update exit-code lists decide whether a non-zero exit code is acceptable.
pages.scripts.runtime.check_updates.outp: Built-in parsers inspect the command results they need; custom parsers read one selected step, defaulting to the last step.
pages.scripts.runtime.check_updates.pars: Custom package managers need an update regex with packageName and newVersion groups.
pages.scripts.runtime.check_updates.purp: Refreshes package metadata and turns command output into cached update rows.
pages.scripts.runtime.check_updates.step: Steps run in order and stop at the first failed step.
pages.scripts.runtime.detect.exitCodeBeh: Exit code 0 with found means detected; any other result is treated as not detected.
pages.scripts.runtime.detect.outputConsu: The command must exit with 0 and print found on stdout to enable the manager.
pages.scripts.runtime.detect.parserBehav: No update parser is used for detection output.
pages.scripts.runtime.detect.purpose: Determines whether a package manager is available on a remote system.
pages.scripts.runtime.detect.stepBehavio: Detection uses exactly one command so the result is unambiguous.
pages.scripts.runtime.full_upgrade_all.e: A non-zero exit code marks the full upgrade as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.full_upgrade_all.o: Output is streamed live, stored in history, and followed by a recheck.
pages.scripts.runtime.full_upgrade_all.p: No parser configuration is used while upgrading.
Runs the fuller upgrade mode for package managers that support it.
pages.scripts.runtime.full_upgrade_all.s: Full-upgrade commands run as the operation body for the selected manager.
pages.scripts.runtime.list_installed_pac: A non-zero exit code keeps the previous snapshot and marks the refresh as a warning.
The parsed package snapshot is cached per manager; full listing output is not stored in activity history.
Custom package managers need an installed-package regex with packageName and currentVersion groups.
Lists installed packages and their current versions for the system-detail inventory.
Steps run in order and stop at the first failed step.
pages.scripts.runtime.reboot.exitCodeBeh: A non-zero exit code before the reboot command prevents later steps from running.
pages.scripts.runtime.reboot.outputConsu: Output is streamed live and stored in activity history; it is not parsed into system fields or update rows.
pages.scripts.runtime.reboot.purpose: Reboots the remote system after any configured safety checks pass.
pages.scripts.runtime.reboot.stepBehavio: Reboot steps run in order and stop before later steps when an earlier step fails.
pages.scripts.runtime.repair_issue.exitC: A non-zero exit code marks the repair operation as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.repair_issue.outpu: Output is streamed live and stored in activity history; it is not parsed into update rows.
pages.scripts.runtime.repair_issue.purpo: Runs the repair action offered for package-manager issue banners.
pages.scripts.runtime.repair_issue.stepB: The configured repair steps run in order and stop at the first failed step.
pages.scripts.runtime.subtitle: Steps, output, parser, and exit codes
pages.scripts.runtime.system_info.exitCo: A non-zero exit code marks system-info collection as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.system_info.output: The built-in parser reads dashboard sections; custom section mapping reads named output sections into system fields.
pages.scripts.runtime.system_info.parser: Use built-in mode for copied standard scripts, or sectioned mode for custom output.
pages.scripts.runtime.system_info.purpos: Collects OS, kernel, uptime, resource, boot, and reboot-required details.
pages.scripts.runtime.system_info.stepBe: System-info steps run in order and their output is consumed by the configured mapping mode.
pages.scripts.runtime.title: Runtime behavior
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_all.exitCo: A non-zero exit code marks the upgrade as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_all.output: Output is streamed live, stored in history, and followed by a recheck.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_all.parser: No parser configuration is used while upgrading.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_all.purpos: Installs all available updates for one package manager.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_all.stepBe: Upgrade commands run as the operation body for the selected manager.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_selected.e: A non-zero exit code marks the selected-package upgrade as failed.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_selected.o: Output is streamed live, stored in history, and followed by a recheck.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_selected.p: No parser configuration is used while upgrading selected packages.
Upgrades the packages selected by the user.
pages.scripts.runtime.upgrade_selected.s: Selected package placeholders are resolved immediately before SSH execution.
pages.scripts.savedParserStepNoLongerExi: The saved parser step no longer exists. Choose an existing step before saving this script.
pages.scripts.savedParserStepOutsideCurr: The saved parser step is outside the current step list.
pages.scripts.systemInfoMappingDescripti: Use the built-in parser for copied standard scripts, or map custom output section names into dashboard system fields.
pages.scripts.updateParserDescription: Use named regex groups to turn the selected step output into update rows. Required groups are packageName and newVersion; other step output stays in activity history.
pages.settings.areYouSureYouWantToRemove: Are you sure you want to remove this passkey? You will no longer be able to use it to sign in.
pages.settings.areYouSureYouWantToRevoke: Are you sure you want to revoke this API token? Any integrations using it will stop working.
pages.settings.copyThisTokenNowItWillNot: Copy this token now. It will not be shown again.
pages.settings.keepsOnlyThisManyRecentAc: Keeps only this many recent activity entries per system. Older history is automatically deleted, and the same limit is used on the system detail page.
pages.settings.minimumCharactersMustIncl: Minimum 8 characters, must include uppercase, lowercase, and a digit
pages.settings.name: Name
pages.settings.noPasskeysRegisteredAddOn: No passkeys registered. Add one to enable passwordless login.
pages.settings.registerAPasskeyOrConfigu: Register a passkey or configure SSO before disabling password login.
pages.settings.settingsSavedButOidcConfi: Settings saved, but OIDC configuration failed: {error}
pages.settings.showANoticeWhenASystemCon: Show a notice when a system connects as root instead of a dedicated non-root SSH user.
pages.settings.showsDashboardAndSystemWa: Shows dashboard and system warnings this many days before a detected distribution reaches end of life.
pages.settings.useApiTokensToAccessDashb: Use API tokens to access dashboard data from external tools (e.g. homepage widgets). Tokens cannot access management endpoints (settings, auth, passkeys).
pages.settings.usesYourBrowserPreferredL: Uses your browser preferred language for the UI by default and falls back to English when a language is not available. Server-generated text such as notifications and scheduled messages is translated only when you choose an explicit language here; Browser default uses English for those messages.
pages.settings.usesYourBrowserPreferredT: Uses your browser preferred time format by default.
pages.settings.whenEnabledOnlyPasskeyAnd: When enabled, only Passkey and SSO login methods are available.
pages.setup.minimumCharactersWithUpperca: Minimum 8 characters, with uppercase, lowercase, and a digit
